summary.L1_headline: ESPR – Acquires Corstasis for $75M upfront plus milestones. | 03/03/26
summary.L2_brief: Esperion Therapeutics entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Corstasis Therapeutics for $75 million in upfront cash consideration plus up to $180 million in regulatory and commercial milestone payments, along with royalty payments on future product sales. The transaction is expected to close by May 1, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions, and adds Enbumyst to Esperion's portfolio targeting the congestive heart failure market.

L3_detailed.event: Esperion entered into a merger agreement to acquire Corstasis Therapeutics, with Corstasis becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of Esperion upon closing
key_figures: $75,000,000 upfront cash consideration (subject to customary adjustments)
Up to $180,000,000 in contingent milestone payments (regulatory approval and commercial sales)
Additional royalty and licensing-revenue-derived payments on future product sales
L3_detailed.deal_implications: This is Esperion as acquirer, not target; transaction expands pipeline into congestive heart failure with Enbumyst; standard deal structure with customary closing conditions suggests moderate closing certainty
remaining_conditions: Must close by May 1, 2026 outside date
risks_flagged: Forward-looking statements note risk of failure to consummate the Corstasis transaction
Risk of failure to achieve anticipated sales of Enbumyst
Contingent consideration dependent on regulatory approvals and commercial milestones

summary.L2_brief: Benjamin Looker, General Counsel of Esperion Therapeutics, sold 1,689 shares at $2.882 per share on January 20, 2026, totaling approximately $4,868. This was a mandatory sale to cover tax obligations on vesting RSUs rather than a discretionary sale, and thus does not signal negative sentiment about the company's prospects.

L3_detailed.pattern_notes: This is a tax-related sale tied to RSU vesting, not a discretionary disposition. The sale represents less than 0.5% of the insider's total holdings, indicating no material reduction in position.
risks_flagged: Sale was for tax withholding on RSU vesting – not discretionary

summary.L1_headline: ESPR – Preliminary 2025 results and 2026 guidance released. | 01/11/26
summary.L2_brief: Esperion Therapeutics issued preliminary 2025 results and 2026 financial guidance ahead of the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference. The company expects 2026 operating expenses of $210-$245 million, with R&D expenses of $40-$50 million and SG&A of $170-$195 million. This is a routine operational update with no merger or deal implications.

L3_detailed.event: Esperion released preliminary 2025 results and fiscal year 2026 financial guidance via press release and management presentation for the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference
L3_detailed.deal_implications: No merger or acquisition activity disclosed; this is a standalone operational and financial update
risks_flagged: Forward-looking statements subject to uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially

summary.L1_headline: + ESPR – Sheldon L. Koenig sold 48,244 shares. | 12/17/25
summary.L2_brief: Esperion CEO and Director Sheldon Koenig disposed of 48,244 shares at $3.673 per share (~$177,200 total) on December 17, 2025. The sale was made to satisfy tax obligations on vesting restricted stock units, representing a routine administrative transaction rather than a discretionary sale. Koenig retains substantial holdings of 1,470,587 shares post-transaction.

L3_detailed.pattern_notes: This is a tax-related sale tied to RSU vesting, not a discretionary disposal. The sold shares represent approximately 3.2% of the insider's post-transaction holdings, indicating no significant reduction in ownership stake. This is a routine administrative transaction commonly seen with equity compensation.
risks_flagged: Sale was for tax obligation satisfaction on RSU vesting - not discretionary
No 10b5-1 plan indicated for this transaction
